purpose: Explainable first-pass policy for routing managed local-worker loops after P50-P53.

decision_rules.trigger: any supervisor-owned subtask lacks start/end token checkpoints
decision_rules.rationale: Delegation economics are token-cost economics, not wall-clock timing.

decision_rules.trigger: worker input/output token counts are unavailable for a provider-backed run
decision_rules.rationale: Quality-only evidence cannot support token-economics tuning.
Self-audit and repair outputs that cannot preserve source IDs are not auditable cheaply.
A local self-auditor that misses known calibration defects should not drive repair.

decision_rules.trigger: tool call, invalid tool call, or tool execution event is observed
decision_rules.rationale: Tool behavior changes the authority and risk class of the benchmark.

decision_rules.trigger: source chunk, page anchor, document ID, or SHA evidence is missing
decision_rules.rationale: Source-level audit cannot be inferred from worker prose.

decision_rules.trigger: inputs are sanitized summaries and source-level audit is not requested
decision_rules.rationale: P50 showed this task shape can reduce paid reporting-review cost, though margins were small.

decision_rules.trigger: corpus registry, chunk manifest, source SHA, and runtime chunk roots exist
decision_rules.rationale: P53 prepared the next high-volume task lane; quality and economics remain unmeasured at corpus scale.

model_scope_guidance.guidance: Use as candidate extractor on bounded source chunks; require record ID preservation and supervisor calibration before scaling.
Promising for reporting; not yet proven for self-audit or repair. Treat SDK tool behavior as explicit lane evidence.
